name: aptitude
description: High-level interface to the APT package manager

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install package(s)

options.name: --no-upgrade
options.description: Do not upgrade packages

options.name: --only-upgrade
options.description: Do not install new packages

name: --auto-remove
--autoremove
options.description: Acts like running the autoremove command

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove package(s)

name: --auto-remove
--autoremove
options.description: Acts like running the autoremove command

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.description: Same as remove, but also deletes configuration files

subcommands.name: hold
subcommands.description: Mark the package to be ignored by future safe-upgrade or full-upgrade commands

subcommands.name: unhold
subcommands.description: Mark to package to no longer be ignored by future safe-upgrade or full-upgrade commands

subcommands.name: keep
subcommands.description: Cancels any scheduled actions on the package

subcommands.name: reinstall
subcommands.description: Re-install packages that are already installed and at the newest version

subcommands.name: markauto
subcommands.description: Mark packages as automatically installed

subcommands.name: unmarkauto
subcommands.description: Mark packages as manually installed

subcommands.name: build-depends
subcommands.description: Satisfy the build dependencies of a package

subcommands.name: forbid-version
subcommands.description: Forbid a package from being upgraded to a particular version

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update all packages

options.name: --no-allow-insecure-repositories
options.description: Forbid the update command to acquire unverifiable data from configured sources

subcommands.name: safe-upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade installed packages, without removing any packages

options.name: --no-new-installs
options.description: Do not install new packages to resolve dependencies

subcommands.name: full-upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade installed packages, removing or installing packages as necessary

options.name: --with-new-pkgs
options.description: Allow installing new packages when used in conjunction with upgrade

subcommands.name: dist-upgrade
subcommands.description: Same as upgrade, but also handles changing dependencies with new versions of packages

subcommands.name: keep-all
subcommands.description: Cancels all scheduled actions on all packages

subcommands.name: forget-new
subcommands.description: Forgets all internal information about which packages are 'new'

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Searches for packages matching one of the patterns given

name: -F
--display-format
options.description: Specify the format which should be used to display output from the search and versions commands

options.name: --disable-columns
options.description: Causes output to show without any special formatting

name: -O
--sort
options.description: Specify the order in which output should be displayed

name: -w
--width
options.description: Specify the display width which should be used for output

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Displays detailed information about package(s)

subcommands.name: versions
subcommands.description: Displays the versions of the packages given

name: -F
--display-format
options.description: Specify the format which should be used to display output from the search and versions commands

options.name: --disable-columns
options.description: Causes output to show without any special formatting

options.name: --group-by
options.description: Control how the output is grouped

name: -O
--sort
options.description: Specify the order in which output should be displayed

options.name: --show-package-names
options.description: Controls when to show package names

subcommands.name: add-user-tag
subcommands.description: Adds a user tag to the selected group of packages

subcommands.name: remove-user-tag
subcommands.description: Removes a user tag from the selected group of packages

subcommands.name: why
subcommands.description: Explains the reason that a package should be installed

options.name: --show-summary
options.description: Summarize each dependency chain instead of displaying it in long form

subcommands.name: why-not
subcommands.description: Explains the reason that a package cannot be installed

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Removes all previously downloaded .deb files from the package cache directory

subcommands.name: autoclean
subcommands.description: Removes any cached packages which can no longer be downloaded

subcommands.name: changelog
subcommands.description: Downloads and displays the Debian changelog for each of the given sources/binary packages

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Downloads the .deb file for the given package to the current directory

subcommands.name: extract-cache-subset
subcommands.description: Copy the apt configuration directory and a subset of the package database to the specified directory

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Displays a brief summary of the available commands and options

options.name: --add-user-tag
options.description: Add the user tag to all packages that are installed, removed, or upgraded

options.name: --add-user-tag-to
options.description: Add the user tag to all packages that match the pattern

options.name: --allow-new-upgrades
options.description: Allow the dependency resolver to install upgrades for packages (when the safe resolver is being used)

options.name: --allow-new-installs
options.description: Allow the safe-upgrade command to install new packages

options.name: --allow-untrusted
options.description: Install packages from untrusted sources without prompting

name: -D
--show-deps
options.description: Show brief explanations of automatic installations and removals

name: -d
--download-only
options.description: Download packages to the package cache as necessary, but do not install or remove anything

options.name: -f
options.description: Try hard to fix the dependencies of broken packages

options.name: --full-resolver
options.description: Use the default 'full' resolver to solve package dependency problems

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display a brief help message

options.name: --log-file
options.description: Log messages will be written to the given file (or standard output if - is given)

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Only log messages at the givben level or higher

options.name: --log-resolver
options.description: Set some standard log levels related to the resolver, to produce logging output suitable for processing with automated tools

options.name: --no-new-upgrades
options.description: Forbid the dependency resolver from installing upgrades (when the safe resolver is being used)

options.name: --no-show-resolver-actions
options.description: Do not display the actions performed by the 'safe' resolver

options.name: -o
options.description: Set a configuration file option directly

name: -P
--prompt
options.description: Always display a prompt before downloading, installing, or removing packages

options.name: --purge-unused
options.description: Purge packages that are no longer required by any installed package, removing their configuration files and perhaps other important data

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Suppress all incremental progress indicators

name: -R
--without-recommends
options.description: Do not treat recommendations as dependencies when installing new packages

name: -r
--with-recommends
options.description: Treat recommendations as dependencies when installing new packages

options.name: --remove-user-tag
options.description: Remove the user tag from all packages that are installed, removed, or upgraded by this command

options.name: --remove-user-tag-from
options.description: remove the user tag from all packages that match the given pattern

name: -s
--simulate
options.description: Print the actions that would normally be performed but don't actually perform them

options.name: --safe-resolver
options.description: Use a 'safe' algorithm to solve package dependency problems

options.name: --schedule-only
options.description: Schedule operations to be performed in the future, but don't perform them

options.name: --show-resolver-actions
options.description: Display the actions performed by the 'safe' resolver

name: -t
--target-release
options.description: Set the release from which the package should be installed

name: -V
--show-versions
options.description: Show which versions of packages will be installed

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Display extra information

options.name: --version
options.description: Display the version of aptitude and information about how it was compiled

options.name: --visual-preview
options.description: Start up the visual interface when installing/removing packages

options.name: -W, --show-why
options.description: Show what manual package is reponsible for each automatically installed/removed package

name: -y
--assume-yes
options.description: Answer yes to all prompts

options.name: -Z
options.description: Show how much disk space will be used/freed by the packages being installed/upgraded/removed
